title: Deploying Sandbox Containers
concept: Managing pools of warm, isolated Docker containers to execute agent code instantly with minimal cold-start delay.

exercises.prompt: What is a main difference between stateless and stateful workers?

options.text: Stateless workers remember your previous actions.
Statefull workers can maintain user sessions across requests.

exercises.explain: Stateful workers can keep track of user sessions, while stateless workers do not retain any previous user actions.

cards.title: What are Docker Containers?
cards.body: Docker containers are lightweight, portable environments for running applications. Think of them as small, self-sufficient boxes that hold everything needed to run a piece of software.

cards.title: What is a Sandbox Container?
cards.body: A sandbox container is an isolated environment where you can execute code safely without affecting other applications or systems.

cards.title: Containers are like Hotel Rooms
cards.body: Imagine each Docker container as a hotel room. Each room (container) can host guests (apps) independently. Even if one room is messy, the others remain clean and unaffected!

cards.title: Deploying a Container
cards.body: When you deploy a Docker container, it's like quickly reserving a hotel room for an event. You can have many rooms ready, so there’s no waiting or delays when guests (agents) arrive.

cards.title: Containers are Complex to Manage
cards.body: Many think managing Docker containers is hard, but once set up, it's more efficient than managing virtual machines and allows for quick deployment.

cards.title: Comparing Cold and Warm Starts
cards.body: A cold start takes time to set up a new environment, while a warm start uses an already prepared container.

right.text: Ready to use instantly, no delays!

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes a warm container?

options.text: A container that needs to be started from scratch.
A container that is already running and ready to execute code.
A container that is unchanged and static forever.
A container that cannot run any code.

exercises.explain: A warm container is already running, allowing for instant code execution without delays.

exercises.prompt: Warm containers have a delay when executing code.
exercises.explain: False. Warm containers are ready and can execute code instantly.

exercises.prompt: A _____ container is already prepared, allowing for instant execution of agent code.
exercises.sentence: A warm container is already prepared, allowing for instant execution of agent code.
exercises.explain: The blank must be 'warm', which means the container is ready to execute without delays.

exercises.prompt: Which statement was generated by the AI?

options.text: Sandbox containers are like having pre-cooked meals ready to eat.
Sandbox containers can only store food temporarily.
Once containers are closed, they can never be reopened.

exercises.explain: The correct option is comparing sandbox containers to pre-cooked meals, illustrating their instant availability for use.

exercises.prompt: Which benefit of using warm Docker containers is the most significant?

options.text: They require less memory than cold containers.
They can execute agent code instantly, reducing wait time.
They are more secure than cold containers.
They are cheaper to create than cold containers.

exercises.explain: The most significant benefit of warm containers is their ability to execute code instantly, minimizing delays and improving efficiency.
